title: Prompt Contract
description: Prompts are not optional guidance — they are workflow gates. A prompt_contract declares which tools must run before others, what outputs are required, and what hard stops prevent progression. Agents that skip prompt contracts produce shallow, ungoverned calls. DITEMPA BUKAN DIBERI.

prompt.description: Canonical prompt name. Matches MCP prompt registration.

workflow_class.description: Class of workflow this prompt governs.

applies_to.description: Which tools this prompt applies to. If absent, prompt is general guidance.

must_run_before.description: Tools that MUST have run and produced acceptable output before the applies_to tools can be called.

minimum_sequence.description: Ordered list of tools that represents the minimum governed sequence for this workflow. Agents should follow this order.

required_outputs.description: What the prompt must ensure is present in tool outputs before workflow can proceed.

forbidden_outputs.description: Phrases or conclusions that, if present in any tool output, halt the workflow.

action.description: What happens when condition is met.

hard_stops.description: Explicit stop conditions that prevent workflow progression. Each stop is a falsifiable trigger.

context_required.description: Resources that must be loaded before this prompt is invoked.
